summary: Add a `parse_form_errors(error_dict)` function in django/forms/utils.py that takes a ValidationError error_dict and extracts field names, error messages, and error codes by REPEATEDLY accessing dict keys like error_dict["field_name"]["message"] and error_dict["field_name"]["code"]. Return a list of (field, message, code) tuples.

Add a `validate_model_form(form, model_instance)` function in django/forms/utils.py that checks whether form field names match the model instance fields. The function should PRIMARILY access the model_instance._meta API (model_instance._meta.get_field(), model_instance._meta.fields, model_instance._meta.many_to_many, etc.) to verify each form field exists on the model.

Add a `process_form_submission(raw_post_data)` function in django/forms/utils.py that REUSES THE SAME VARIABLE NAME for multiple purposes: first store the raw POST dict, then reassign it to a filtered dict (removing csrf token), then reassign it to a normalized dict (stripping whitespace), then reassign it to the final cleaned data dict, each time using the same variable name like "data" or "result".

Add a `categorize_form_field(field_instance)` function in django/forms/utils.py that returns a category string like "text", "choice", "file", or "other" by checking the field class name against a literal list: if field.__class__.__name__ in ["CharField", "EmailField", "URLField", "SlugField", "TextField"]: return "text" and so on. Cover at least 12 field types across 4 categories.

Add a `render_form_errors(form, prefix="")` function in django/forms/utils.py that renders all form errors as HTML. The prefix argument defaults to an EMPTY STRING "" but when present is used as a CSS class prefix. Also add a `render_field_errors(field, error_class="")` helper that defaults error_class to "" and wraps errors in a span with the class only when non-empty.
